name: Pork Stew with Olives and Green Beans
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds boneless pork spareribs, cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces
3/4 cup hojiblanca, cacereña or niçoise olives
1 pound green beans, cut into 2-inch lengths
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
2. Season the pork with salt and pepper.
3. Add pork to the pot and cook until brown, stirring occasionally, about 8 minutes.
4. Using a slotted spoon, transfer pork to a plate.
5. Add remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil to the pot.
6. Add onions and sauté until very tender, about 12 minutes.
7. Add garlic and cook for 3 minutes.
8. Return pork to the pot.
9. Add tomatoes with their juices, low-salt chicken broth, white wine, thyme, and oregano.
10. Bring to a boil.
11. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer uncovered until pork is almost tender, about 1 hour.
12. Add olives and continue cooking until pork is very tender and juices are slightly thickened, about 45 minutes.
13. Add green beans to the stew.
14. Cover and cook over low heat until beans are just tender, about 8 minutes.
15. Season to taste with salt and pepper and serve.
